title: ponera nicaraguensis
intro: Ponera nicaraguensis is a plant species, also known as "Guatemalan Dog's Bane" or "Ponera", which belongs to the Apocynaceae family. The medicinal properties of Ponera nicaraguensis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a subject of interest in traditional medicine. The plant's health benefits are said to include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Ponera nicaraguensis are often made by infusing its leaves in hot water or as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
